zotero.abstractNote: Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (Neural ODEs) have emerged as a powerful framework for modeling continuous-time dynamical systems. However, they often struggle to capture complex, non-stationary dynamics where the underlying system behavior changes over time. To address this, we introduce Modulated Neural ODEs (MoNODEs), a novel architecture that incorporates a modulation mechanism to adapt the vector field of the ODE based on time-varying context. By conditioning the dynamics on a latent representation of the system's state, MoNODEs can effectively model non-stationary processes and exhibit improved generalization capabilities. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a range of synthetic and real-world datasets, showing that MoNODEs outperform standard Neural ODEs and other baselines in terms of predictive accuracy and computational efficiency.
